pterodactyl-tall.motifs: Sky motif differs from the sibling: this page shipped a WARM GOLD SUN in the 3.1 wave while pterodactyl-wide has a CRESCENT MOON (gemini-3.1-migration.md). Nothing enforces motif consistency (ISSUES) — a regen of either page should decide deliberately whether to match.

pterodactyl-wide.motifs: Sky motif differs from the sibling: this page has a CRESCENT MOON while pterodactyl-tall shipped a WARM GOLD SUN in the 3.1 wave (gemini-3.1-migration.md). Nothing enforces motif consistency (ISSUES) — a regen of either page should decide deliberately whether to match.

retry.notes: FIX THE EYES (most important): the pupil polarity is backwards in this input — the LARGE oval disc filling most of each eye reads SOLID WHITE, which is WRONG and renders as a glowing blank orb. Erase the white inside each pupil and make the pupil interior BLACK (the dark board showing through), keeping only a thin white outline ring around the pupil and the small round catchlight dot SOLID WHITE inside the pupil. Fill the sclera — the thin crescent between the eyeball outline and the pupil ring — SOLID WHITE. Never leave a pupil as one solid white disc: every finished eye must read as a white sclera around a BLACK pupil with a single small white catchlight. Do not add teeth, dots, or any other new marks.

chalk.review: Solid-pen-eye page: the pen paints the pupil as solid ink, so the 2026-07 chalk copied it and the composited night eye was a blank white orb (solid chalk ink renders white in dark mode — the police-wide/horse-wide class). findEyeCores finds no cores here, so the eye-polarity gate passes VACUOUSLY (the 'eye whites not chalked (1)' warning is expected and survivable). Render the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE and confirm a legible dark pupil before trusting any chalk of this page. After a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES fix: the 2026-07-13 night-fill-only regen (PR #142) could NOT fix this — the white orb is the chalk's own solid-ink pupil, which no fill change can darken. The erase-and-redraw note above (t 0.4) turned the pupil into a fillable dark region with white sclera + catchlight (applied take 1, keep 100% / local 98.5%); night then regenerated against it + re-punched.

retry.notes: paint each eye pupil interior deep near-black so it reads as a solid dark pupil under the white sclera and catchlight the line art already provides; keep the whole head and muzzle the same green as the body; paint the back plates, round back spots, and tail spikes plain flat warm amber-gold with no vein lines or patterns; the dinosaur body is green, never navy or blue

night.review: Conditions on the 2026-07-14 fixed chalk (fillable dark pupil). Confirm on the composite: a solid near-black pupil under the white sclera and catchlight, green head/body/muzzle, flat amber-gold plates/spots/spikes with no veins, no navy body. Sample 2 of 3 shipped (drift 0.0000, bgLuma 39, lineW 255; bgLuma golden re-frozen 34.7 → 39.4, within the ≤60 gate). audit-fill-eyes passes regardless — judge on the composite.
night.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES fix — night regenerated against the fixed chalk with body/plate color pins. The earlier 'NO white catchlight / crescent pupil' note (this session, 2026-07-13) was written for the OLD solid-ink chalk that left only a sliver of fillable pupil; it is superseded now that the regenerated chalk provides a real fillable dark pupil the fill paints near-black.

Erase the white inside each pupil and make the pupil interior BLACK (the dark board showing through), keeping only a thin white outline ring around the pupil and the small round catchlight dot SOLID WHITE inside the pupil. chalk.review: Solid-pen-eye page: the pen paints the pupil as solid ink, so the 2026-07 chalk copied it. The pre-fix composite read as a brown ring around a white core — the fill could paint only the thin crescent the chalk left, while the chalk's solid-ink pupil centre still glowed white. findEyeCores finds no cores here, so the eye-polarity gate passes VACUOUSLY. Render the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE and confirm a legible dark pupil before trusting any chalk of this page. After a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES fix: PR #142's 2026-07-13 night-fill-only regen darkened the fillable crescent but left the chalk's solid-ink pupil centre white. The erase-and-redraw note above (t 0.4, 3 tries) rebuilt the chalk eye as white sclera + fillable dark pupil + small white catchlight (keep 99.9% / local 97.7%); night then regenerated against it + re-punched.

retry.notes: paint each eye pupil interior deep near-black so it reads as a solid dark pupil under the white sclera and catchlight the line art already provides; keep the raptor a moonlit green body with its darker green stripes, never navy or blue; the open mouth interior is deep warm red with the teeth left white as the line art draws them

night.review: Conditions on the 2026-07-14 fixed chalk (fillable dark pupil). Confirm on the composite: a solid near-black pupil under the white sclera and centred catchlight, moonlit-green body with darker green stripes, warm-red open mouth with white teeth (not invented — the pen has them). Sample 2 of 3 shipped (drift 0.0000, bgLuma 30, lineW 255; night lineWhite golden 184 → 249, cleaner chalk lines). audit-fill-eyes passes regardless — judge on the composite.
night.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES fix — night regenerated against the fixed chalk with identity color pins. The earlier 'solid dark pupil … with only a small white catchlight' note (2026-07-13) was aimed at the OLD solid-ink chalk and shipped a still-white-cored eye; superseded now that the chalk provides a real fillable pupil.
